text_blocks.text: using captured enemies as body shields. After
several days only a few remaining soldiers,
loyal supporters of the Shah, held out in
the citadel. After the fortress fell,
Genghis supposedly reneged on his surrender
terms and executed every soldier that had
taken arms against him at Samarkand. The
people of Samarkand were ordered to
evacuate and assemble in a plain outside the
city, where they were killed and pyramids of
severed heads raised as a symbol of
victory. Ata-Malik Juvayni, a high official in
the service of the Mongol empire, wrote that
in Termez, on the Oxus, "all the people,
both men and women, were driven out onto
the plain, and divided in accordance with
their usual custom, then they were all slain".
